findings.summary: GPU solver code (gpu_beam_search.py, gpu_sequential_solver.py, live_gpu_stream.py) has zero unit tests
findings.detail: The three most complex files -- gpu_beam_search.py (2266 lines), gpu_sequential_solver.py (1450 lines), and live_gpu_stream.py (2388 lines) -- have no unit tests. Even CPU-testable functions like cpu_verify(), pre_simulate_locked(), SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks construction could be tested with CPU-only mocks.

findings.summary: At least 15 Python files are stale solver generations without DEPRECATED markers
findings.detail: The codebase contains multiple solver generations no longer in the active pipeline but still present without deprecation markers: beam_search.py, optimizer.py, seq_solver.py, smart_policy.py, order_solver.py, trip_solver.py, pipeline_solver.py, two_phase_planner.py, gpu_optimizer.py, gpu_sim.py, parallel_optimizer.py, planner_optimizer.py, solve.py, solve_and_save.py, solve_all.py, solve_from_capture.py, pipeline.py. Only planner_v2.py and planner_v3.py have DEPRECATED notices.

findings.summary: refine_from_solution uses raw kwargs while solve_sequential migrated to SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks
findings.detail: solve_sequential() accepts SolveConfig and SolveCallbacks dataclass objects, but refine_from_solution() still uses 15 individual keyword arguments. The docstring says 'Other args same as solve_sequential' but the actual interface differs.

findings.summary: Difficulty detection logic duplicated in JS endpoints and Python configs.py
findings.detail: iterate/+server.js detectDifficultyFromLog() and optimize/replay/+server.js both hardcode bot-count-to-difficulty mapping that duplicates configs.py detect_difficulty(). Creates maintenance risk if configs change.
